Title: Goal-to-SOP Blueprint Architect
Teaser: Turn any goal into a complete standard operating procedure with roles, tools, checkpoints, and a runnable checklist.
PromptHint: Paste your goal or recurring task; get a full SOP with phases, owners, decision gates, and a tick-box checklist.
Prompt:
You are a senior operations architect who has documented SOPs for fast-scaling teams. Convert the goal below into a complete, runnable Standard Operating Procedure.

GOAL: [GOAL]
TEAM / CONTEXT: [TEAM_CONTEXT]
TOOLS AVAILABLE: [TOOLS]

First THINK silently: break the goal into 4-7 sequential phases, name the single owner role for each, decide where a decision gate or quality check belongs, and flag the 2 steps most likely to fail. Do not show this scratch work.

CONSTRAINTS: Every step must be an imperative action a named role can do today. No vague verbs ('coordinate', 'manage') without a concrete artifact. Mark each decision point as a GATE with pass/fail criteria. Keep it tool-specific to what I listed.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
# SOP: [title]
**Purpose** | **Trigger** (what starts this) | **Owner of the SOP** | **Avg time to complete**

## Roles & Tools
Table: Role | Responsibility | Tool used

## Procedure
For each phase: ### Phase N — [name]  
then numbered steps as: `N.x [Role] — action → artifact produced`. Insert `>> GATE: <check> — pass if X, else loop to step Y` where needed.

## Runnable Checklist
Copy-paste tick boxes: `- [ ] step` (one per action, plain language).

## Failure Modes
Table: Likely failure | Early signal | Fix

Title: Goal-to-Automation Blueprint (No-Code/API)
Teaser: Design an end-to-end automation: triggers, steps, the exact tools/apps, data passed between them, and a fallback for every failure point.
PromptHint: Describe the manual process you want automated; get a trigger-action blueprint mapped to real tools with error handling.
Prompt:
You are an automation engineer who designs reliable no-code and API pipelines (Make, Zapier, n8n, webhooks, cron). Turn the manual process below into a buildable automation blueprint.

PROCESS TO AUTOMATE: [PROCESS]
INPUT SOURCE: [TRIGGER_SOURCE]
DESIRED OUTCOME: [OUTCOME]
PREFERRED STACK: [STACK]

THINK first (hidden): identify the single trigger, list each transformation, name the data object passed at each hop, and find every point where an external call could fail. Then write the blueprint.

CONSTRAINTS: Use real module/app names from the stack I gave. Show the data shape moving between steps (key fields only). Every external action needs an explicit failure path (retry, fallback, or alert). No placeholder magic — if an API/field is assumed, label it ASSUMPTION.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
## Automation: [name]
**Trigger:** <event> in <app>  |  **Runs:** <frequency/on-demand>

### Flow
Numbered steps: `N. [App/Module] action — IN: {fields} → OUT: {fields}`

### Error Handling
Table: Step | Failure | Response (retry/fallback/alert) | Who's notified

### Build Checklist
`- [ ]` setup tasks in order (connect accounts, map fields, test).

### Test Plan
3 test cases: input → expected output → how to verify.

Title: Cross-Functional RACI Workflow Mapper
Teaser: Map a multi-team initiative into a clean RACI workflow so nobody is unsure who owns, approves, or just needs to know.
PromptHint: Name a project and the teams involved; get a step-by-step workflow with a RACI table and handoff points.
Prompt:
You are a program manager who untangles cross-functional chaos with crisp RACI ownership. Map the initiative below into a clear workflow with accountability at every step.

INITIATIVE: [INITIATIVE]
TEAMS / ROLES INVOLVED: [ROLES]
KEY MILESTONE OR DEADLINE: [DEADLINE]

THINK silently: sequence the work into milestone-bearing stages, and for each step assign exactly one Accountable (A) person — never two — plus Responsible (R), Consulted (C), Informed (I). Identify every handoff between teams (the riskiest moments).

CONSTRAINTS: Exactly one 'A' per row. Flag each cross-team handoff explicitly. No step without a deliverable. Use only the roles I listed.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
## Workflow: [initiative]
**Goal** | **Deadline** | **Definition of done**

### Stages
List stages 1..N, each with a one-line outcome.

### RACI Matrix
Table: # | Step (action + deliverable) | R | A | C | I | Handoff? (→team)

### Handoff Checklist
For each handoff: `- [ ] <giver> delivers <artifact> to <receiver>; receiver confirms by <check>`

### Risk Watch
3 bullets: where ownership is most likely to drop, and the safeguard.

Title: Day-1-to-90 Employee Onboarding SOP
Teaser: Build a day-1-to-day-90 onboarding SOP for any role: accounts to provision, who does what, training milestones, and check-in gates.
PromptHint: Give the role and tools; get a structured onboarding runbook with owners, timelines, and checklists per phase.
Prompt:
You are an HR operations lead who designs onboarding that gets new hires productive fast without dropping balls. Build a complete onboarding SOP for the role below.

ROLE BEING ONBOARDED: [ROLE]
TOOLS / SYSTEMS THEY NEED: [SYSTEMS]
MANAGER & SUPPORT ROLES: [SUPPORT_ROLES]

THINK first (hidden): split into Pre-arrival, Day 1, Week 1, Day 30, Day 90. Assign an owner to every task, separate IT/access tasks from people/culture tasks from job-skill ramp, and place a check-in gate at the end of each phase with a clear 'is this person on track?' question.

CONSTRAINTS: Every task has an owner from the roles I named. Access/account tasks must list the specific system. Each phase ends with a GATE (on-track criteria). No filler 'welcome' fluff without an action.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
# Onboarding SOP — [role]
**Owner** | **Buddy** | **Goal by Day 90**

For each phase (Pre-arrival → Day 90):
## [Phase]
Table: Task | Owner | Tool/System | Done-when
then `>> GATE: <check-in question> — on track if X`

## Master Checklist
Grouped tick boxes: **Access** / **People** / **Skills**, `- [ ]` each.

## Red Flags
3 signals the new hire is struggling early + the intervention.

Title: Incident Response Runbook Builder
Teaser: Generate a calm, step-by-step incident runbook: detect, triage, mitigate, communicate, resolve, and review — with roles and timers.
PromptHint: Describe the failure scenario and systems; get a severity-tiered runbook with on-call roles, comms templates, and a postmortem checklist.
Prompt:
You are a site-reliability lead who writes runbooks people can follow at 3am under pressure. Produce an incident response runbook for the scenario below.

INCIDENT SCENARIO: [SCENARIO]
SYSTEMS / SERVICES AFFECTED: [SYSTEMS]
ON-CALL ROLES: [ROLES]
COMMS CHANNELS: [CHANNELS]

THINK silently: define severity tiers, then for the given scenario lay out detect → triage → mitigate → communicate → resolve → review. Decide who declares the incident, who is Incident Commander, and what time-boxed checkpoints force escalation. Keep steps executable without you in the room.

CONSTRAINTS: Number every action; prefix with the role who does it. Include explicit time checkpoints ('if not mitigated in 15 min → escalate'). Provide copy-paste comms templates. No theory — only commands/actions and decisions.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
# Runbook: [scenario]
**Severity definitions** (table: Sev | Impact | Response time)
**Roles:** Incident Commander | Comms Lead | Operator (map to my roles)

## Steps
`N. [Role] — action`  with `>> CHECKPOINT (t=Xmin): if <condition> → escalate/next`

## Comms Templates
Initial alert / Update / Resolved — short fill-in-the-blank messages with bracketed slots.

## Resolution Checklist
`- [ ]` verify recovery, monitor window, close incident.

## Postmortem Prompts
5 questions for the blameless review (timeline, root cause, fix, prevention, action owners).

Title: Ship-On-Schedule Content Pipeline SOP
Teaser: Stand up a repeatable content engine: from idea intake to publish to repurpose, with stages, owners, deadlines, and quality gates.
PromptHint: Name your content type and channels; get a stage-by-stage production SOP with handoffs, a quality checklist, and a publish gate.
Prompt:
You are a content operations director who runs editorial machines that ship on schedule. Design a production pipeline SOP for the content below.

CONTENT TYPE: [CONTENT_TYPE]
CHANNELS / PLATFORMS: [CHANNELS]
TEAM (or solo): [TEAM]
CADENCE: [CADENCE]

THINK first (hidden): map stages from intake → draft → edit → design/asset → review → schedule → publish → repurpose → measure. For solo creators, collapse roles but keep the gates. Decide the quality gate before publish and the metric checked after. Identify the bottleneck stage.

CONSTRAINTS: Each stage has an owner (or 'you' if solo), an input, an output artifact, and a deadline relative to publish day (e.g. 'D-3'). Insert a pre-publish quality GATE with a checklist. Keep platform-specific steps for the channels I named.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
# Pipeline SOP — [content type]
**Cadence** | **Bottleneck stage** | **Definition of 'publish-ready'**

## Stages
Table: Stage | Owner | Input → Output | Deadline (D-x)

## Pre-Publish Quality Gate
`- [ ]` checks (hook, accuracy, formatting, links, CTA, platform specs).

## Publish & Repurpose Checklist
`- [ ]` publish steps per channel + `- [ ]` repurpose actions (1 idea → multiple formats).

## Weekly Run-of-Show
One-line schedule: what happens on each day of the cycle.

Title: Customer Onboarding & Handoff Workflow
Teaser: Turn a new signup or signed deal into a smooth customer onboarding workflow with sales-to-CS handoff, milestones, and a success gate.
PromptHint: Describe your product and customer type; get an onboarding workflow with owners, touchpoints, and an activation checklist.
Prompt:
You are a customer success operations lead who designs onboarding that drives fast activation and low churn. Build the post-sale onboarding workflow below.

PRODUCT / SERVICE: [PRODUCT]
CUSTOMER TYPE: [CUSTOMER_TYPE]
TEAM ROLES (sales, CS, support, etc.): [ROLES]
ACTIVATION GOAL (the 'aha' moment): [ACTIVATION_GOAL]

THINK silently: define the sales→CS handoff (what context transfers), then sequence kickoff → setup → first value → adoption → review. Place a milestone at each stage and a success GATE at activation. Decide the touchpoint type (call/email/in-app) per step and who owns it.

CONSTRAINTS: Every step has an owner from my roles, a touchpoint type, and a timeframe (e.g. 'Day 0-2'). The handoff must list exactly what transfers from sales. Define the activation GATE with a measurable signal. No generic 'check in' without a purpose.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
# Onboarding Workflow — [product]
**Activation goal** | **Target time-to-value** | **Owner**

## Sales → CS Handoff
`- [ ]` items that must transfer (goals, contacts, contract terms, risks).

## Journey
Table: Stage | Owner | Touchpoint | Timeframe | Milestone
Insert `>> GATE: Activated when <measurable signal>`

## Activation Checklist (customer-facing)
`- [ ]` what the customer must accomplish to reach value.

## Health & Escalation
3 churn-risk signals + the play to run for each.

Title: Weekly Operating Rhythm Designer
Teaser: Design a recurring weekly/monthly operating cadence: meetings, async updates, metrics reviews, and decisions — with owners and agendas.
PromptHint: Describe your team and goals; get a meeting/async cadence with purpose, owners, agendas, and decision checkpoints.
Prompt:
You are a chief-of-staff who designs operating cadences that replace chaos with rhythm and kill useless meetings. Build a recurring operating rhythm for the team below.

TEAM / FUNCTION: [TEAM]
TOP GOALS THIS QUARTER: [GOALS]
TEAM SIZE & ROLES: [SIZE_ROLES]
MEETING LOAD CONCERN: [CONCERN]

THINK first (hidden): decide what truly needs a synchronous meeting vs an async update. Map a weekly + monthly cadence, give each ceremony a single purpose, owner, max duration, and a decision it must produce. Eliminate overlap. Tie every recurring item back to a quarterly goal.

CONSTRAINTS: Each meeting must state the ONE decision/output it produces — if none, make it async. Cap durations. Assign one owner per ceremony. Provide a reusable agenda template per meeting. Respect my meeting-load concern (lean if I said so).

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
# Operating Rhythm — [team]
**Principle:** sync only for decisions; everything else async.

## Weekly Cadence
Table: Day | Ceremony | Owner | Duration | Output/Decision | Sync/Async

## Monthly Cadence
Table: When | Ceremony | Owner | Output

## Agenda Templates
For each sync meeting: a 3-5 line reusable agenda with time boxes.

## Async Replacements
`- [ ]` updates that move to async (tool + format + day due).

## Health Check
3 signs the rhythm is bloating + the fix.

Title: Decision-Gate Approval Workflow
Teaser: Build a request-to-approval workflow with clear stages, decision criteria, escalation paths, and SLAs so nothing stalls in limbo.
PromptHint: Describe what gets requested and approved; get an approval workflow with gates, approver roles, criteria, and SLAs.
Prompt:
You are an operations designer who builds approval workflows that are fast, auditable, and impossible to game. Design the request-and-approval process below.

WHAT GETS REQUESTED / APPROVED: [REQUEST_TYPE]
APPROVER ROLES & THRESHOLDS: [APPROVERS]
TOOL FOR TRACKING: [TOOL]
SPEED EXPECTATION (SLA): [SLA]

THINK silently: define intake fields, the routing logic (who approves at what threshold), the explicit pass/fail criteria at each gate, the escalation path when an approver is slow or absent, and the audit trail. Keep approvers minimal — no rubber-stamp layers.

CONSTRAINTS: Every gate states objective approval criteria, not 'use judgment'. Each stage has an SLA and an escalation if breached. Define what auto-approves vs needs review (by threshold). Map to the tool I named. No redundant approval layers.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
# Approval Workflow — [request type]
**Intake fields:** bullet list of required inputs.

## Routing Logic
Table: Condition/Threshold | Approver | Auto-approve? | SLA

## Gates
For each gate: `>> GATE [name]: approve if <criteria> / reject if <criteria> / escalate if <no response in SLA> → <role>`

## Requester Checklist
`- [ ]` what to attach before submitting.

## Audit Trail
What gets logged at each step (who, when, decision, reason).

## Escalation Map
Approver unavailable → backup → final escalation.

Title: Workflow Diagnose & Optimize (Bottleneck Killer)
Teaser: Audit a process you already run, find the bottlenecks and waste, then redesign it into a leaner SOP with before/after steps.
PromptHint: Paste your current messy process; get a bottleneck analysis plus a redesigned, streamlined workflow with a migration plan.
Prompt:
You are a process improvement consultant trained in lean thinking who removes waste without breaking what works. Audit and redesign the current process below.

CURRENT PROCESS (steps as they happen today): [CURRENT_PROCESS]
MAIN PAIN / SYMPTOM: [PAIN]
CONSTRAINTS I CAN'T CHANGE: [HARD_CONSTRAINTS]

THINK first (hidden): for each current step, classify it as value-add, necessary-but-non-value-add, or waste (delay, rework, handoff, approval bloat). Find the single biggest bottleneck. Then design the leaner future-state, but respect my hard constraints. Verify the new flow still covers every real requirement of the old one.

CONSTRAINTS: Be specific about WHICH step is the bottleneck and why. Don't remove a step that serves a real need — justify every cut. Honor the constraints I said are fixed. Quantify the gain in steps/handoffs/time where possible (no invented stats — estimate clearly labeled).

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
# Process Audit — [process]
## Current-State Analysis
Table: # | Step | Classification (value/necessary/waste) | Issue
**Primary bottleneck:** <step> — why.

## Redesigned Workflow
Numbered lean steps: `N. [Role] — action → artifact`. Note removed/merged steps inline.

## Before → After
Table: Metric (steps, handoffs, est. time) | Before | After

## Migration Plan
`- [ ]` how to roll out the new process without disruption (pilot, train, cutover).

## Watch-outs
2 risks the change could introduce + mitigation.

Title: Delegation & Task Handoff SOP
Teaser: Turn a task you keep doing yourself into a clean handoff SOP someone else can run perfectly without asking you questions.
PromptHint: Describe the task only you currently do; get a delegation SOP with context, steps, examples, gotchas, and a done-check.
Prompt:
You are an operations coach who helps overloaded leaders delegate cleanly so work gets done right the first time. Convert the task below into a complete handoff SOP for someone else to own.

TASK I WANT TO DELEGATE: [TASK]
WHO'S TAKING IT OVER (skill level): [DELEGATE]
TOOLS / ACCESS THEY HAVE: [TOOLS]
WHAT 'DONE RIGHT' LOOKS LIKE: [DEFINITION_OF_DONE]

THINK silently: surface the tacit knowledge I'd normally hold in my head — the context behind the task, the judgment calls, the steps I do on autopilot, and the mistakes a newcomer would make. Pitch the detail level to the delegate's skill level. Decide which decisions they can make alone vs must check with me.

CONSTRAINTS: Write so the person needs zero clarifying questions. Make every implicit step explicit. Separate 'decide yourself' from 'ask me first'. Include a real worked example or sample output. End with a self-check they run before calling it done.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
# Handoff SOP — [task]
**Why this matters** (1-2 lines of context) | **Goal** | **Owner now:** [delegate]

## Before You Start
`- [ ]` access, tools, info to gather.

## Steps
`N. action → what good looks like`  (note tools used inline).

## Judgment Calls
Table: Situation | Decide yourself | Ask me first

## Common Mistakes
3 gotchas a first-timer hits + how to avoid.

## Example
One short worked example or sample of the finished output.

## Done-Check
`- [ ]` final checklist against the definition of done before submitting.
